title: Dill-Infused Malaysian Chuck Roast Biryani
description: A refreshing, post-workout Malaysian dish featuring chuck roast, shio-koji cure, and vegan-friendly ingredients.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together shio-koji cure, dill, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the cure mixture all over the chuck roast, cover, and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Heat coconut oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the chuck roast on all sides until browned, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the roast from the pot and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium and add onion, ginger, and curry powder to the pot.
Cook, stirring occasionally, until the onion is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the chickpea broth and lemon juice to the pot, stirring to combine.
Return the chuck roast to the pot, cover, and bring to a boil.
Transfer the pot to the oven and braise the chuck roast for 2.5 hours, or until tender.
While the roast is cooking, prepare the basmati rice according to package instructions.
To assemble the biryani, place a portion of rice on a plate, followed by sliced chuck roast, and garnish with fresh dill.
